title: Gendos singularity and Virtual Reality
wikitext:
[]: Gendo's singularity & Virtual Reality             Quote Post by Ukago » Wed Jan 07, 2015 6:38 am Year 2015 is marked as the year of virtual reality. Consumer version of Oculus is going to be released and the of year, as well as final movie. I realy belive that this technology is going to change the world and it's the beginning of true singularity. Gendo's vizor look like a VR device - so my conclusion is that changes in the plot are actualy made for for that purpose - that final impact, merging of all consciousnesses will be part VR collective experience, allowing to see Gendo's singularity through his "eyes" What do you guys think?

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Jan 07, 2015 7:02 am I think this belongs in the Chit Chat Section. Also, I'm eagerly awaiting Gendou Rift Merchandise.

[]:             Quote Post by Ukago » Wed Jan 07, 2015 7:26 am Not necessarily. If Gendo's vizor is truly connecter to VR, this could also be a hint to his master plan. Merging and moving into new virtual universe inside big earth-core?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jan 07, 2015 8:59 am It could simply be that the visor is some sort of interface so Gendo could control all the automated system on which neo-NERV's facilities entirely run and/or maybe he simply received some damages in the eyes during the time skip and had to have that visor installed to be able to see. Now on a symbolic level the visors have a clear purpose: the eyes are the "windows of the soul", the most expressive part of our faces, Gendo's shaded glasses in Jo and Ha already made it difficult to see his expression (and thus what he thinks) and symbolized how his heart was mostly closed to everything and everyone around him. The visor in Q make this symbolism of how Gendo closed his heart go even further, where we can't even see his eyes anymore and aren't even sure if they are still here beneath the visor. It make him look even less human, which is fitting for a guy living in a floating almost empty ruin and surrounded by machines (and Fuyutsuki)

[]:             Quote Post by Ukago » Wed Jan 07, 2015 9:27 am All these factors don't exclude eachother. When you get into VR you will see what this technology realy means. It's not just 'movies' and games. It's a paradigm shift. Controlling devices with VR - already happening.. .That's my point. Singularity is a point to witch everything flows into - technology, biology and our consciousness. It's our gate to next level of reality.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jan 07, 2015 9:46 am Ukago wrote: Controlling devices with VR - already happening.. .That's my point.

[]:             Quote Post by Ukago » Wed Jan 07, 2015 10:34 am :) also if reality has a fractal construction, it is realy possible that reality created in VR affects the "outer" reality. All these loop theories, tesseract and ect. suggest that it might be the Finals message: You are the creator, as in EoE but with much wider connection to the real world. :) So if Gendo wants to replace God...

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Fri Jan 09, 2015 4:40 pm You should have choosed Project Morpheus and not Occulus for the title......

[]:             Quote Post by Ukago » Sun Jan 11, 2015 3:36 am Dima wrote: You should have choosed Project Morpheus and not Occulus for the title......

[]:             Quote Post by The Polyethylene Man » Thu Jan 22, 2015 7:15 am According to the wiki for the original proposal, Gendo wanted to digitize himself as part of instrumentality. http://wiki.evageeks.org/Resources:Neon_Genesis_Evangelion_Proposal_%28Translation%29

[]:             Quote Post by one-eyed » Fri Jan 23, 2015 11:14 am I never understood the obsession in the 90s on collective mind and Lotus Eater Machines as upper stage of the human species: the Borg Collective, the Taelon Commonality, the Matrix, the Instrumentality, etc. Personally I think these forms of disgusting and fascist Transhumanism: I remember the practices attributed to the cult of hashashin (the cult of the Assassins) who (allegedly) used hashhash (meaning opium), prostitutes and beautiful gardens to create the "secret garden of paradise" and "motivate" its members to a suicidal fanaticism because they thought they would live in the "Paradise of the 77 Virgins" when they died. It would be like considering addicted to Crack and Opium the higher stages of the human species. I think we would become entheogen cultists. Humankind has progressed because we are different as oil and water, each with its function. If it were possible to force the merger probably would not have a potable fuel, we would have a liquid that is not for drinking and that does not burn and it would be useless and polluting. Collective minds should not work because every train will at the speed of its slowest vehicle and I do not even want to imagine that obscenities and madness lurk in the minds of Gendo, Kihl and alike. The alternative would be to create individual havens for every human being, where every human live alone forever with his fantasies and dreams fulfilled. This seems to me the kind of paradise that Gendo and Anno would like. In recent years many "villains" in anime has advocated this idea: Madara in Naruto, Osaburo Kaiba in Yu-Gi-Oh, etc. All show how wrong this idea, and in Evangelion, I always thought that Anno was an ardent supporter of it and the whole story criticizing otaku by fleeing reality is pure hypocrisy and covardice to accept his true beliefs. Someone who finds the right to control my mind, body, soul and destiny by knowing what is "best for everyone" is my enemy and should be treated as such and without mercy. Neither gods were so assholes like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jan 23, 2015 11:26 am one-eyed wrote: I never understood the obsession in the 90s on collective mind and Lotus Eater Machines as upper stage of the human species

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Jan 23, 2015 11:36 am ^ I love Last and First Men . And what's more, at the end they're sending out seeds of human life - just like the FAR!

[]:             Quote Post by Ukago » Sat Jan 24, 2015 2:57 am one-eyed wrote: I never understood the obsession in the 90s on collective mind and Lotus Eater Machines as upper stage of the human species: the Borg Collective, the Taelon Commonality, the Matrix, the Instrumentality, etc. Personally I think these forms of disgusting and fascist Transhumanism...
